author: Antigravity Agent
funFacts: The sea urchin feeds primarily on kelp, acting as a critical regulator of coastal kelp forest density.
Sea sea urchins have round, spike-covered shells (tests) made of calcium carbonate plates, protecting them from hungry fish.
